title: 🧩subworkflow - dynatrace create dashboard
description:
Subworkflow to create a dashboard.

This Subworkflow is used to create a dashboard
Use Case example, create a Investigation Dashboard
when Davis Raises a Problem, for Teams to easily
access the Data needed to solve the problem.

Parameters:

dashboardname: (Required) name of dashboard to be created
dashboardjson: (Required) Json def. of dashboard to be created

1.title: Problem and Remediation Events

3.query:
fetch  events
| filter dt.davis.is_duplicate==false
| filter matchesValue(event.name, "Process memory Saturation") or matchesValue(event.name, "Process CPU Saturation") or matchesValue(event.name, "Low Storage Warning")
| filter matchesPhrase( entity_tags, "remediation:on")  
| filter matchesValue(event.kind, "DAVIS_PROBLEM") and matchesValue(event.status, "ACTIVE") 
| summarize countDistinct(display_id), alias: Number_of_open_davis_problems
//| fields concat( Number_of_open_davis_problems," 🧠 ")

7.query:
fetch events
| filter matchesValue(event.name, "Process memory Saturation") or matchesValue(event.name, "Process CPU Saturation") or matchesValue(event.name, "Low Storage Warning")
| filter matchesValue(event.kind, "DAVIS_PROBLEM")  and matchesValue(event.status, "CLOSED") 
| filter matchesPhrase( entity_tags, "remediation:on")  
| filter matchesPhrase(display_id, "*P*") 
| summarize average_open_time = avg(resolved_problem_duration)

9.query:
fetch  events
| filter matchesValue(event.name, "Process memory Saturation") or matchesValue(event.name, "Process CPU Saturation") or matchesValue(event.name, "Low Storage Warning")
| filter dt.davis.is_duplicate==false
| filter matchesPhrase( entity_tags, "remediation:on")  
| filter matchesValue(event.kind, "DAVIS_PROBLEM") and matchesValue(event.status, "CLOSED") 
| summarize countDistinct(display_id) , alias: Number_of_open_davis_problems
//| fields concat( Number_of_open_davis_problems," 🧠 ")

12.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter matchesPhrase(event.kind, "workflow")
| filter matchesPhrase(dt.automation_engine.workflow.title, "dyn.usecase-aprs-aws-ssm-remediate-cpu-memory-saturation-parent") or matchesPhrase(dt.automation_engine.workflow.title, "dyn.usecase-aprs-aws-ssm-remediate-low-storage-warning-parent") or matchesPhrase(dt.automation_engine.workflow.title, "dyn.usecase-aprs-aws-ssm-service-now-notification")
| filter matchesValue(event.type, "WORKFLOW_EXECUTION")
| filter matchesValue(dt.automation_engine.state, "RUNNING")
| sort start_time
| summarize count()

13.query:
fetch events
| filter   matchesPhrase(event.name, "*Closed Service Now Incident")
| summarize (countDistinct(event.id) * tolong($man_hr_per_incident)), alias: nr_of_automations

14.query:
fetch events
| filter   matchesPhrase(event.name, "*Closed Service Now Incident")
| summarize (countDistinct(event.id) * tolong($man_cost_per_incident)), alias: nr_of_automations

18.query:
timeseries { max(dt.process.cpu.usage), value.A = avg(dt.process.cpu.usage, scalar: true) }, by: { dt.entity.process_group_instance }, filter: { matchesValue(entityAttr(dt.entity.host, "tags"), "*[AWS]Name:Dynatrace-SSM-Action-Demo*") }
| fieldsAdd dt.entity.process_group_instance.name = entityName(dt.entity.process_group_instance)
18.title: Process CPU Usage

19.query:
timeseries { max(dt.process.memory.usage), value.A = avg(dt.process.memory.usage, scalar: true) }, by: { dt.entity.process_group_instance }, filter: { matchesValue(entityAttr(dt.entity.host, "tags"), "*[AWS]Name:Dynatrace-SSM-Action-Demo*") }
| fieldsAdd dt.entity.process_group_instance.name = entityName(dt.entity.process_group_instance)
19.title: Process memory Usage

21.title: Disk Usage

56.query:
fetch  events
| filter dt.davis.is_duplicate==false
| filter matchesValue(event.name, "Process memory Saturation") or matchesValue(event.name, "Process CPU Saturation") or matchesValue(event.name, "Low Storage Warning")
| filter matchesPhrase( entity_tags, "remediation:on")  
| filter matchesValue(event.kind, "DAVIS_PROBLEM") and matchesValue(event.status, "ACTIVE") 
| lookup [
fetch dt.entity.ec2_instance
| filter matchesPhrase(tags,"Dynatrace-SSM-Action-Demo")
| fieldsadd cpuusage = lookup([ timeseries max = max(dt.host.cpu.usage), by:{dt.entity.host} 
| fieldsadd awsname=entityAttr(dt.entity.host, "awsNameTag"), cpumax = arrayLast(max)] , sourceField:entity.name, lookupField:awsname)
| fieldsadd memusage = lookup([ timeseries max = max(dt.host.memory.avail.percent), by:{dt.entity.host} 
| fieldsadd awsname=entityAttr(dt.entity.host, "awsNameTag"), memmax = arrayLast(max)] , sourceField:entity.name, lookupField:awsname)
| fieldsadd diskusage = lookup([ timeseries max = max(dt.host.disk.used.percent), by:{dt.entity.host} 
| fieldsadd awsname=entityAttr(dt.entity.host, "awsNameTag"), diskmax = arrayLast(max)] , sourceField:entity.name, lookupField:awsname)
| fieldsadd entity.name,regionName,awsInstanceType, HostCpuUsage=cpuusage[cpumax], HostMemoryUsage=100 - memusage[memmax],HostDiskUsage=diskusage[diskmax],awsInstanceId,awsSecurityGroup, awsVpcName, arn, tags, id, amiId,virtualizedDiskType, awsBasicMonitoring, localHostName, publicHostName
| fieldsadd  nr=0.1 + random() 
| fieldsadd latitude=if(regionName == "us-east-1", 42.600833 + nr, else:if(regionName == "us-east-2", 40.0946354 + nr)), longitude=if(regionName == "us-east-1", -111.456667 + nr, else:if(regionName == "us-east-2", -82.7541337 + nr))],sourceField:host.name[0], lookupField:localHostName
|  fields display_id, host=lookup.entity.name, region=lookup.regionName, event.category, event.name, event.description, event.start, instance_id=lookup.awsInstanceId, ami=lookup.amiId, arn=lookup.arn, instance_type=lookup.awsInstanceType, VPC=lookup.awsVpcName, root_cause_entity_name, ip=lookup.localHostName, lat=lookup.latitude, long=lookup.longitude, HostCpuUsage=lookup.HostCpuUsage, HostMemoryUsage=lookup.HostMemoryUsage,HostDiskUsage=lookup.diskusage ,problem_link=concat("https://kyw96254.apps.dynatrace.com/ui/apps/dynatrace.davis.problems/problem/",event.id), Classic_Problem_link=concat("https://kyw96254.apps.dynatrace.com/ui/apps/dynatrace.classic.problems/#problems/problemdetails;gtf=-2h;gf=all;pid=",event.id),
host_link=concat("https://kyw96254.apps.dynatrace.com/ui/apps/dynatrace.infraops/hosts/",dt.entity.host[0]), problem_id=concat(concat(display_id," - "), event.name)
56.title: Problems Map

59.query:
fetch events
| filter matchesPhrase(event.name, "Service Now Incident*") and matchesPhrase(event.name, "created*")
//| filter   matchesPhrase(event.name, "Service Now Incident*")
| summarize countDistinct(event.name), alias: nr_of_automations

60.query:
fetch events
| filter   matchesPhrase(event.name, "*Closed Service Now Incident")
| summarize countDistinct(event.id) , alias: nr_of_automations

guide:
## 📊 **Subworkflow to Create a Dashboard**

🧩 This subworkflow provides a simple and automated way to **create a dashboard** using a predefined JSON definition.

It is commonly used to **dynamically create investigation or troubleshooting dashboards** as part of automation workflows.

---

### 🎯 **Use Case**

A typical use case is to **automatically create an investigation dashboard when a problem is raised**, allowing teams to quickly access all relevant data needed to analyze and resolve the issue.

This helps:
- 🔍 Centralize relevant metrics and data
- ⚡ Reduce time to investigation
- 👥 Enable teams to collaborate using a shared, ready-to-use dashboard

---

### ⚙️ **Parameters**

- **dashboardname** (Required): 🏷️ The name of the dashboard to be created.  
- **dashboardjson** (Required): 🧾 The JSON definition of the dashboard to be created.

---

### 🧠 **Workflow Logic**

- 📤 The subworkflow submits the provided dashboard JSON definition
- 📊 A new dashboard is created with the specified name
- ✅ If creation is successful, the subworkflow completes
- ❌ If creation fails, the subworkflow fails—allowing the **parent workflow to retry or take alternative action**

---

### 🧩 **Usage Context**

- ✅ Commonly used in **incident or problem response workflows**
- ✅ Enables on-demand creation of investigation dashboards
- ✅ Works well with automated remediation and validation steps
- ✅ Reduces manual dashboard setup during critical situations

---
